title: Psychotria calliantha
intro: Psychotria calliantha, also known as Kalamansi, is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria calliantha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Psychotria calliantha is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used as a tincture, to take advantage of its reported health benefits.
taxonomy: Psychotria calliantha is classified in the family Rubiaceae, which includes over 13,000 species of flowering plants commonly known as coffee and gardenias. This family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. The genus Psychotria consists of over 1,800 species of tropical plants, many of which are used in traditional medicine. Psychotria calliantha is a species of flowering plant native to Madagascar and is known for its unique flowers and leaves.
